title: ROM memory inference and initialization
body:
Starting from merge of #843 it is not possible to force Yosys to infer a ROM memory from a verilog array initialized using `initial` statement(s).

From my understanding the goal of #843 was optimization so small ROMs are always implemented using LUTs. That makes sense. However in my case I want to place a CPU program inside an initialized BRAM and I want the BRAM to be inferred rather instantiated explicitly.

Please find a test case attached: [bram-init.zip](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/2957512/bram-init.zip) This is a ROM memory with firmware intended to be used by the PicoSoC.

I suppose that there should be kind of a "threshold" so the Yosys can decide: If a memory is ROM and is smaller than X bytes then use LUTs, otherwise make it a ROM memory.
